name: Scallop Sauté with Winter Vegetables
recipeIngredient: 1 inch knob unpeeled fresh ginger, thinly sliced
2 inch knob unpeeled fresh ginger, finely chopped
1 tablespoon dark red oriental sesame oil
recipeInstructions:
1. Heat a dry wok, heavy skillet, or sauté pan for 1 to 2 minutes.
2. Add 1 tablespoon of oil and heat over high flame until hot but not smoking, about 1 to 2 minutes.
3. Place the first batch of ginger and garlic in the hot oil, stir, and remove after 1 minute. Discard ginger and garlic.
4. Toss the scallops in the flour mixture just before cooking.
5. Add 1 tablespoon of oil to the wok and heat until very hot. Place half the scallops in the oil and stir-fry for 45 seconds to 1 minute. Remove and place on a warm plate.
6. Add another tablespoon of oil, heat, and cook the remaining scallops. Remove.
7. Add the remaining oil, heat, stir in half the vegetables (except ginger and garlic), and stir-fry for 30 seconds to 1 minute. Remove.
8. Reheat the oil and stir-fry the remaining vegetables. Remove.
9. Let the oil reheat slightly, add ginger and garlic, and cook for 1 minute.
10. Add sesame oil along with already cooked scallops and vegetables. Stir-fry about 1 minute, tossing to reheat and mix ingredients evenly. Add salt and pepper. Serve immediately.
Notes: All ingredients in stir-fry dishes must be very thinly and uniformly sliced (1/4-inch widths) to cook evenly in less than a minute. Small shrimp, already-cooked lobster slices, or monkfish may be substituted.
